Title: The squeeze cell hypothesis for the activation of jasmonate synthesis in response to wounding

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Octadecanoid pathway; formation of 13-hydroperoxide jasmonate precursors
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies precursor pool and promotes de novo jasmonic acid synthesis distal to wounds
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: Polyunsaturated fatty acids, likely α-linolenic acid
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Oxygenates polyunsaturated fatty acids to produce 13-hydroperoxide intermediates that feed jasmonate biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control point for rapid distal wound-induced jasmonate accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Defense signaling in response to wounding and other environmental stresses
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Leaf vascular-associated tissues distal to wounds; cells adjacent to mature xylem vessels
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Promoter expressed in a distinct niche of cells adjacent to mature xylem vessels (putative contact cells)
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expressed in cells tightly associated with mature xylem vessels in leaves distal to wounds
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Mutant analysis and wound-induced jasmonate profiling in distal tissues; promoter expression analysis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Necessary for much of the rapid accumulation of jasmonic acid in tissues distal to wounds
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Wounding; herbivory; distal leaf response within about 90 s after wounding another leaf
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Manipulation of LOX6 may alter wound-induced jasmonate production and defense signaling, but likely with pleiotropic effects on stress responses and growth-defense balance
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: This is a hypothesis/review paper. The paper states LOX6 is necessary for much of rapid distal JA accumulation based on cited studies; it also proposes that LOX6 may decode GLR-dependent long-distance signals and may be activated directly or indirectly by Ca2+ fluxes
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. LOX6 is identified as a lipoxygenase necessary for much of the rapid jasmonic acid accumulation in tissues distal to wounds. 2. LOX6 promoter activity marks cells adjacent to mature xylem vessels, implicating xylem-associated contact cells as sites of early distal JA synthesis. 3. LOX6 likely functions directly or indirectly downstream of GLR-dependent long-distance wound signaling to promote de novo jasmonate synthesis.

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Octadecanoid pathway; formation of fatty acid 13-hydroperoxide jasmonate precursors
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Produces fatty acid 13-hydroperoxide precursors feeding jasmonate biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Defense signaling in response to wounding and environmental stresses
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Leaf tissues including mesophyll-derived synthesis sites
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supports jasmonate precursor formation in mesophyll cells
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Mentioned as contextual/background evidence that cells outside vasculature produce JA; not a primary core focus of this article
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. LOX2 is cited as encoding an enzyme producing fatty acid 13-hydroperoxide jasmonate precursors. 2. Its strong mesophyll expression supports extravascular JA synthesis. 3. In this paper, it serves mainly as background context rather than the central experimental focus.

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Clade 3 GLUTAMATE RECEPTOR-LIKE protein; ligand-binding domain, three transmembrane domains, pore loop, amino-terminal domain
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Direct or indirect control of ion fluxes and wound-induced electrical signaling upstream of jasmonate synthesis
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Long-distance activation of jasmonate synthesis in response to wounding
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Wounding; herbivory; axial and radial pressure changes; possible mechanostimulation; glutamate and other amino acid/glutathione agonists from cited work
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: LOX6-associated jasmonate synthesis module; distal jasmonate pathway activation; JAZ10 expression
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Promotes distal jasmonate synthesis and distal JAZ10 induction via upstream signaling
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Required as part of clade 3 GLR signaling for full distal wound-induced jasmonate pathway activation
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Likely functions in homo- or heteromeric GLR channel complexes; specific subunit composition not resolved
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Predicted/likely vascular expression; paper proposes some clade 3 GLRs may be expressed in contact cells and at least one in phloem
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: glr3.3 glr3.6 double-mutant analysis; electrophysiology; distal jasmonate signaling readout via JAZ10 expression
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Loss contributes to reduced distal jasmonate signaling and diminished wound-activated surface potential changes
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Large crush wounds; herbivory by Spodoptera littoralis; mechanostimulation-related hypotheses
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Potential broad effects on electrical signaling, development, and stress responses if manipulated
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Could be targeted to alter systemic wound signaling and jasmonate-mediated defense, but mechanistic uncertainty and likely pleiotropy warrant caution
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: This is a review/hypothesis article summarizing prior work. GLR3.3 is genetically linked upstream of jasmonate synthesis rather than being a canonical transcriptional regulator
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GLR3.3 is part of the clade 3 GLR-dependent long-distance wound signaling system upstream of jasmonate synthesis. 2. In the glr3.3 glr3.6 mutant, distal leaves show reduced jasmonate signaling and diminished wound-activated surface potential changes. 3. GLR3.3 may regulate ion fluxes, possibly Ca2+, that activate LOX6-dependent jasmonate synthesis.

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Clade 3 GLUTAMATE RECEPTOR-LIKE protein; ligand-binding domain, three transmembrane domains, pore loop, amino-terminal domain
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Direct or indirect control of ion fluxes and wound-induced electrical signaling upstream of jasmonate synthesis
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Long-distance activation of jasmonate synthesis in response to wounding
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Wounding; herbivory; xylem pressure changes; possible mechanoperception
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: LOX6-associated jasmonate synthesis module; distal jasmonate pathway activation; JAZ10 expression
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Promotes distal jasmonate synthesis and downstream jasmonate-responsive gene activation
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Required together with GLR3.3 for full distal wound-induced jasmonate pathway activation
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Likely functions in GLR channel complexes with other clade 3 GLRs; specific subunit composition unresolved
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Likely vascular; at least one of GLR3.3/GLR3.6 is inferred to be expressed in phloem
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: glr3.3 glr3.6 double-mutant analysis; electrophysiology; distal JAZ10 response assays
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Loss contributes to reduced distal jasmonate signaling and abolished herbivore-induced sieve tube depolarization in the double mutant context
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Potential pleiotropic effects on systemic signaling and other GLR-mediated processes
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential leverage point for modifying systemic defense activation, but strong caution due to broad signaling roles
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: This review emphasizes GLR3.6 as part of a clade 3 GLR pathway upstream of LOX6 and jasmonate synthesis
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GLR3.6 functions with GLR3.3 in a clade 3 GLR pathway controlling long-distance wound signaling. 2. The double mutant displays reduced distal jasmonate signaling and altered electrical responses. 3. GLR3.6 is proposed to mediate ion fluxes that enable LOX6-dependent rapid JA accumulation distal to wounds.
